meridian_skill.description: Evolve your brain's schema pack. Add page types, propose new ones from corpus scans, backfill page.type on existing pages, audit pack health. Triggers when an agent notices untyped pages, custom domains needing typed entities (researcher, contract, deposition), or wants to see what types the pack declares.
meridian_skill.name: schema-author

outline.text: Phase 1 — Brain (know which pack is active)
Phase 2 — Assess (what does the current pack cover?)
Phase 3 — Propose (what types should the pack add?)
Phase 5 — Sync (backfill existing pages with the new types)

sections.heading: Non-goals (use these other skills instead)
lines: use judgment to follow the Non-goals (use these other skills instead) guidance:
This skill AUTHORS the schema pack (adds page types, link verbs, prefixes,
flags). For these adjacent jobs, route elsewhere:
item: **Filing one specific page** → `skills/brain-taxonomist/SKILL.md`. Brain-
taxonomist routes at WRITE TIME ("where does this note go?"). schema-author
changes the rules at AUTHORING TIME ("what types and prefixes exist?")
item: **Schema-check as part of EIIRP iteration** → `skills/eiirp/SKILL.md`
already has a schema-check phase. Don't duplicate
item: **Just looking up a type's settings** → `gbrain schema explain <type>`
directly. This skill is for CHANGING the pack, not READING from it
item: **Querying who knows about X** → `skills/expert-routing/SKILL.md` (or
`gbrain whoknows` directly). schema-author makes a type expert-routable;
it does not run the query

sections.heading: When to invoke
lines: use judgment to follow the When to invoke guidance:
Invoke when the user (or a sibling skill) says any of:
item: "Add a `researcher` type to my schema"
item: "I have 4000 untyped pages under `meetings/`"
item: "My brain doesn't know that `journal-article` is a type"
item: "Set `paper` to be extractable"
item: "Propose types from what I've ingested"
item: "Sync the new types to backfill existing pages"
DON'T invoke for "where does THIS note go" (use brain-taxonomist) or
"who knows about X" (use expert-routing / `gbrain whoknows`)

sections.heading: Tutorial + vision
lines: **Why this matters:** [`docs/what-schemas-unlock.md`](../../docs/what-schemas-unlock.md) — 7 killer use cases (4000 invisible meetings made queryable, founder ops brain, research brain, legal brain, team brain, agent-as-co-curator) plus the structural argument for why types matter at query time. Read this before pitching schema authoring to a user — it's the doc that explains the difference between a pile of notes and a brain with structure

**5-minute walkthrough:** [`docs/schema-author-tutorial.md`](../../docs/schema-author-tutorial.md) — fork the bundled pack, add a researcher type, sync, prove the T1.5 wiring via `gbrain whoknows`. Use placeholder pages so it runs against any brain without affecting real content

sections.heading: Phase 1 — Brain (know which pack is active)
lines: use judgment to follow the Phase 1 — Brain (know which pack is active) guidance:
codeblock:plain:Z2JyYWluIHNjaGVtYSBhY3RpdmUgLS1qc29u
Output gives you `pack_name`, `version`, `sha8`, `page_types_count`, `source_tier`
If `source_tier === "default"`, the user is on bundled `gbrain-base` and any
mutation will need a fork first (Phase 4)

sections.heading: Phase 2 — Assess (what does the current pack cover?)
lines: codeblock:plain:Z2JyYWluIHNjaGVtYSBzdGF0cyAtLWpzb24=
Returns per-type page counts, untyped count, and `dead_prefixes` (pack-
declared prefixes with zero matching pages — probable mis-declarations)
If coverage < 90%, there's untyped content worth typing
codeblock:plain:Z2JyYWluIHNjaGVtYSByZXZpZXctb3JwaGFucyAtLWxpbWl0IDUwIC0tanNvbg==
Untyped pages drilldown. Look for shared path prefixes (e.g. "12 of these
are under `research/papers/`") — those are candidates for a new type

sections.heading: Phase 3 — Propose (what types should the pack add?)
lines: codeblock:plain:Z2JyYWluIHNjaGVtYSBkZXRlY3QgLS1qc29u
Clusters pages by `source_path` and proposes candidate types. Heuristic
only (no LLM call)
codeblock:plain:Z2JyYWluIHNjaGVtYSBzdWdnZXN0IC0tanNvbg==
LLM-refined candidates with confidence scores. Use the top-3 hit rate as
the signal for which to promote

sections.heading: Phase 4 — Apply (mutate the pack)
lines: use judgment to follow the Phase 4 — Apply (mutate the pack) guidance:
If the active pack is bundled (`gbrain-base` or `gbrain-recommended`),
fork it first:
codeblock:plain:Z2JyYWluIHNjaGVtYSBmb3JrIGdicmFpbi1iYXNlIG1pbmUKZ2JyYWluIHNjaGVtYSB1c2UgbWluZQ==
Then add the types one at a time:
codeblock:plain:Z2JyYWluIHNjaGVtYSBhZGQtdHlwZSByZXNlYXJjaGVyIFwKICAtLXByaW1pdGl2ZSBlbnRpdHkgXAogIC0tcHJlZml4IHBlb3BsZS9yZXNlYXJjaGVycy8gXAogIC0tZXh0cmFjdGFibGUgXAogIC0tZXhwZXJ0
For complex multi-mutation refactors (e.g. add a type AND the link verb
that points to it), agents reaching this surface over MCP can use the
batched `schema_apply_mutations` op:

codeblock:jsonl:
Validate before sync:
codeblock:plain:Z2JyYWluIHNjaGVtYSBsaW50IC0td2l0aC1kYg==
The `--with-db` flag opts into the 2 DB-aware rules
(`extractable_empty_corpus`, `mutation_count_anomaly`) that detect
mis-declared types you'd otherwise discover only at runtime

sections.heading: Phase 5 — Sync (backfill existing pages with the new types)
lines: use judgment to follow the Phase 5 — Sync (backfill existing pages with the new types) guidance:
Dry-run first:
codeblock:plain:Z2JyYWluIHNjaGVtYSBzeW5jIC0tanNvbg==
Returns per-prefix `would_apply` counts + sample slugs. If the numbers
look right:
codeblock:plain:Z2JyYWluIHNjaGVtYSBzeW5jIC0tYXBwbHk=
Chunked UPDATE in 1000-row batches; never wedges concurrent writers
Idempotent on re-run (second `--apply` finds nothing to backfill)

sections.heading: Phase 6 — Verify
lines: use judgment to follow the Phase 6 — Verify guidance:
codeblock:plain:Z2JyYWluIHNjaGVtYSBzdGF0cyAtLWpzb24=
Coverage should be ≥95% now. Spot-check the new type:
codeblock:plain:Z2JyYWluIHdob2tub3dzICJtYWNoaW5lIGxlYXJuaW5nIg==
If `researcher` was declared `--expert`, results should include
researcher-typed pages. (The pack-aware wiring at the query path was
added in v0.40.6.0 — pre-v0.40.6 brains silently ignored custom
expert-routed types.)

sections.heading: Phase 7 — Commit (preserve the change)
lines: use judgment to follow the Phase 7 — Commit (preserve the change) guidance:
If the pack is in source control, commit:
codeblock:plain:Y2Qgfi8uZ2JyYWluL3NjaGVtYS1wYWNrcy9taW5lCmdpdCBhZGQgcGFjay5qc29uCmdpdCBjb21taXQgLW0gInNjaGVtYTogYWRkIHJlc2VhcmNoZXIgKyBwYXBlciB0eXBlcyArIGF1dGhvcmVkIGxpbmsiCmdpdCBwdXNo
If the brain daemon is running (`gbrain serve --http`), other processes
pick up the change within 1 second (stat-mtime TTL gate in
loadActivePack — v0.40.6.0 closed the cross-process invalidation gap)

sections.heading: Outputs
lines: Mutated pack file at `~/.gbrain/schema-packs/<name>/pack.{json,yaml}`
Audit row in `~/.gbrain/audit/schema-mutations-YYYY-Www.jsonl` per mutation
`pages.type` backfilled on matching rows after `sync --apply`
Query paths (`whoknows`, `find_experts`) now route through the new
expert types

sections.heading: Contract
lines: checklist:ai-autonomy:

sections.heading: Anti-Patterns
lines: checklist:ai-autonomy:

sections.heading: Output Format
lines: When invoked, this skill produces structured output suitable for both human + JSON consumption:
**Per-mutation result (JSON):**

codeblock:json:eyJzY2hlbWFfdmVyc2lvbiI6IDEsICJwYWNrIjogIm1pbmUiLCAicGF0aCI6ICIvVXNlcnMvLi4uL3BhY2suanNvbiIsICJmb3JtYXQiOiAianNvbiIsICJwcmV2X3NoYTgiOiAiYTFiMmMzZDQiLCAibmV3X3NoYTgiOiAiZTVmNmc3aDgifQ==
**Per-batch result (from `schema_apply_mutations` MCP op):**
codeblock:json:eyJzY2hlbWFfdmVyc2lvbiI6IDEsICJwYWNrIjogIm1pbmUiLCAiYmF0Y2hfaWQiOiAiYmF0Y2gtMTcxNjQ5MTQwMC1hYmMxMjMiLCAibXV0YXRpb25zX2FwcGxpZWQiOiAzLCAicmVzdWx0cyI6IFt7Li4ufSwgey4uLn0sIHsuLi59XX0=
**Stats JSON (per-source + aggregate + dead-prefix hints):**

codeblock:json:
**Sync dry-run JSON:**

codeblock:json:
**Human output (the agent's final summary):**
One line per mutation: `Pack: <name> (<format>)` and `Sha8: <prev> → <new>`
Stats: total pages, typed %, untyped count, per-type breakdown, dead-prefix list
Sync: per-prefix `would_apply`/`applied` count + sample slugs in dry-run mode

On failure, the error envelope follows the standard `StructuredAgentError` shape from `src/core/errors.ts`: `{error, code, message, details?}`. Codes from the mutation primitives: `PACK_NOT_FOUND`, `PACK_READONLY`, `PACK_CORRUPT`, `TYPE_EXISTS`, `TYPE_NOT_FOUND`, `INVALID_PRIMITIVE`, `INVALID_RESULT`, `IO_ERROR`, `STILL_REFERENCED`, `LOCK_BUSY`

sections.heading: Failure modes
lines: `PACK_READONLY` → you tried to mutate `gbrain-base` or `gbrain-recommended`. Fork first
`INVALID_RESULT` → the mutation would create a dangling reference or
prefix collision. The pre-write lint gate caught it. Read the error
message; the lint rule name names the problem
`STILL_REFERENCED` → you tried to remove a type that another type's
`aliases` / `enrichable_types` / `link_types` / `frontmatter_links`
references. The error names every reference. Remove those first
`LOCK_BUSY` → another process is mid-mutation. Wait 30s and retry, or
pass `--force` if you know the holder is wedged
`permission_denied` (MCP only) → your OAuth client doesn't have `admin`
scope. Re-register with `gbrain auth register-client --scopes admin`
